candidate_problems.text: What is the precise, non-metaphorical mechanism linking 'strange-loop' self-reference to the specific mathematical structures that succeed in physics? The conjecture gestures at invariance, compositionality, duality, recursion, and self-reference, but it does not yet explain why these features are load-bearing rather than a retrospective summary of already-successful mathematics. A key open problem is to derive discriminating criteria: given a mathematical domain before physical application, can the conjecture predict its likelihood of deep physical relevance better than simpler alternatives such as symmetry-based selection, historical contingency, or broad mathematical generality?

How can the conjecture be made genuinely testable without collapsing into a flexible after-the-fact narrative? The proposed sharpening predicts that loop-structured mathematics will be disproportionately fruitful in physics, but this needs operational definitions, baselines, and possible falsifiers. Open questions include how to measure 'loop structure' across mathematical fields, how to compare transfer rates into physics, and what counterexamples would count against the view—for example, highly successful physical mathematics lacking obvious self-reference, or richly recursive mathematics with little physical uptake.

Does the conjecture explain the effectiveness of mathematics in physics specifically, or only the broader fact that model-building systems favor reusable abstractions? There is an unresolved tension between a deep ontological claim about reality and a more modest epistemic claim about what cognitive agents can discover and use. If the latter suffices, the conjecture may not explain why the world itself is mathematically structured; if the former is intended, it must clarify how self-modeling minds are relevant to physical law that predated minds. Exploring whether the account is anthropic, epistemic, ontological, or some combination is a central new problem.
